action.reasoning: Observation: the uniform 9-member ConvNeXt weight soup is a new best at 0.7776, substantially improving few-shot accuracy versus logit ensembling. Analysis: this is the first post-hoc method that improved hidden accuracy, suggesting weight averaging is a better fit than output averaging/calibration. We also have 8 additional ConvNeXt checkpoints that hurt as a logit ensemble, but may still help in weight space if selectively included; a greedy model soup using the balanced validation set is a plausible next step and relatively cheap compared with more training. Decision: create a greedy ConvNeXt soup candidate from all available same-architecture ConvNeXt checkpoints, save the best validation soup, and submit it. If it overfits/underperforms, the final iteration will revert to the known-best uniform soup9 checkpoint.
